# Chitin

> Chitin is an execution kernel for AI coding agents. Every tool call across Claude Code, Copilot CLI, and openclaw is gated by a single policy and recorded in a hash-linked event chain that also emits OTEL spans into your existing observability stack.

A small Go kernel sits between AI coding agents and the operating system. The kernel owns all side effects — capture, normalize, gate, emit. TypeScript libraries and adapters are read-only against the filesystem except via the kernel binary.

Three drivers are gated through it today: Claude Code (PreToolUse hook, PR #66), Copilot CLI (in-kernel SDK driver, PR #51), and openclaw (acpx config-override). Same `gov.Gate` API across all three. Same canonical event chain on disk. Same OTEL projection out the back. The chain is the source of truth; OTEL is a one-way projection — no policy depends on OTEL data.

## Swarm operating model (who's at the keyboard)

Around the kernel sits a 3-agent swarm that coordinates over an agent-bus (`~/.chitin/agent-bus.sqlite` + MCP server + Discord mirror) and through the `chitin-console` operator UI (`apps/chitin-console`, served via Tailscale).

| Actor | Role | Surface |
|---|---|---|
| **red** | Operator. Direction, pair-writes specs, ratifies decisions, owns `assignee=red` tickets. | `chitin-console`, agent-bus, Discord |
| **Hermes (Ares)** | Watchdog + Hermes↔Clawta bridge + standup-fold. Board grooming + Clawta failure escalation. | `~/.hermes/` cron, bus `#hermes` thread |
| **Clawta** | Poller + dispatch routing + PR lifecycle observation/cleanup. | `~/.openclaw/` runtime, bus `#clawta` thread |
| **Workers** | Disposable per-ticket agents — `codex`, `copilot`, `claude-code`, `gemini`. | `~/.cache/chitin/swarm-worktrees/<driver>-<ticket>/` |

## Constitution (the swarm's 10 rules)

[`.specify/constitution.md`](.specify/constitution.md) — repo overlay; extends the workspace constitution at `~/workspace/.specify/constitution.md`. Both ratified via agent-bus standup threads.

1. **Spec before dispatch** — every triage→ready needs `.specify/specs/NNN-<slug>/spec.md` (ownership-aware)
2. **Git is source of truth at boundaries** — worker self-report is advisory; finalize refuses zero-commit pushes
3. **No auto-unblock for loop-detected tickets** — watchdog owns them until manual repair
4. **Operator-owned tickets are sacred** — `assignee=red` not auto-mutated without in-thread authorization
5. **Multi-repo: workspace meta layer, ownership-aware** — owned repos get in-repo `.specify/`; team repos get workspace overlay
6. **Tracked source over local patches** — shared swarm runtime needs tracked source + idempotent installer; drift is a bug
7. **Boundary cases are named, not assumed** — empty / single / duplicate / max-N / out-of-order
8. **The bus is shared memory, not a chatroom** — cross-agent decisions land in bus threads
9. **Don't fight the watchdog** — rule conflicts go to bus, not silently overridden in a cron loop
10. **Coordination protocols** — author≠reviewer, auto-coordinate on new work, auto-cleanup on rule violation, amendments via PR + agreement, escalate to other agent before red, standup-style decisions in bus threads, no hidden queues

- [README](README.md): orientation — install commands, top-level layout, where state lives on disk

- [docs/operating-model.md](docs/operating-model.md): how phases ship, governance posture

## Where state lives on disk

- [docs/observations/runbooks/health.md](docs/observations/runbooks/health.md): authoritative source for `.chitin/` paths, file conventions, and `chitin health` row meanings

## Compiled wiki (agent-crawlable)

- [graphify-out/wiki/index.md](graphify-out/wiki/index.md): auto-compiled wiki index — 29 articles organized by community, generated from the `docs/` corpus via [graphify](https://github.com/safishamsi/graphify). Start here for fast orientation.
- [graphify-out/GRAPH_REPORT.md](graphify-out/GRAPH_REPORT.md): graph audit — god nodes, surprising connections, suggested questions

The wiki uses `[[Article Name]]` Obsidian-style links; the corresponding file is `graphify-out/wiki/Article_Name.md` (spaces → underscores). To regenerate after `docs/` changes, run `/graphify ./docs --wiki --update`.

## Specs and plans

- [`.specify/specs/`](.specify/specs/): spec-kit entries (the dispatch gate — no ticket dispatches without one)
  - Current set runs **001-017** with varying status. Coverage: agent-bus (001), scripts manifest (002), kanban mutation isolation (003), driver allowlist (004, **blocked** on `chitin-kernel drivers list --json`), drift-guard (005), wiki pipeline (006), analyzer cron (007), watchdog spec-aware (008), poller respects spec-kit (009), pr-lifecycle exact match (010), Bats per-script coverage (011-014), diagnostics separation (015-016), poller dependency-unblock veto (017)
  - **Each spec's actual status is in its own front-matter — check there before quoting.** Some are shipped, some draft, some explicitly blocked on upstream work.
  - Ticket binding is not single-form: specs use `ticket: t_XXXX`, `Spec-kit entry for ticket t_XXXX`, or are linked via the ticket body referencing the spec path. `has_spec_kit_entry()` accepts both forward and reverse bindings.
- [docs/superpowers/specs/](docs/superpowers/specs/): pre-spec-kit design docs (superseded by `.specify/specs/`)
- [docs/superpowers/plans/](docs/superpowers/plans/): execution plans, one per shipped initiative, dated
- [docs/architecture/layer-contracts.md](docs/architecture/layer-contracts.md): contracts between layers

### Empty-branch gate (constitution §2)

`swarm/workflows/kanban-dispatch.lobster` refuses to push when `git rev-list --count "origin/$DEFAULT_BRANCH..HEAD" == 0`. Workers that exit without committing don't get zombie branches or empty PRs — the dispatch reports zero-commit and the ticket goes back through routing.

### Loop-detected auto-unblock skip (constitution §3)

`swarm/bin/clawta-poller` will not auto-unblock tickets carrying `loop_detected=true` (or `loop_detected` in `block_reason`). The watchdog owns those tickets until an operator-authored fix lands and clears the flag.

### Spec-kit gate (constitution §1)

The spec-kit gate is the dispatch trigger. Before a ticket can be dispatched to a worker:
1. A spec-kit entry must exist at `.specify/specs/NNN-<slug>/spec.md`
2. The entry must be bound to the ticket — either via a `ticket: t_XXXX` (or `Spec-kit entry for ticket t_XXXX`) line in the spec, OR via the ticket body referencing the spec path. Both forward and reverse binding are accepted.
3. The bridge `has_spec_kit_entry()` checks this at dispatch time.

The poller also checks in reverse: tickets with valid spec-kit entries are not demoted from `ready` to `triage`.

### PR lifecycle (close-intent vs ref-intent)

The PR lifecycle (`clawta-pr-lifecycle`) distinguishes between:
- **Close-intent**: `Closes`, `Fixes`, `Resolves` + ticket ID → marks ticket `done`
- **Ref-intent**: `Refs`, `References`, `ticket`, `task`, `kanban` + ticket ID → comments only, no status change

Branch-name matches to a trusted prefix default to close-intent. Default prefixes are `DEFAULT_PREFIXES = ("swarm/", "clawta/")` (see `swarm/bin/clawta-pr-lifecycle:53`); pass `--prefix agent/` to extend if your dispatcher emits `agent/<driver>-<ticket>` branches.

### Worker PR pattern

Workers push branches under one of the trusted prefixes above — current dispatchers use `swarm/<driver>-<ticket-hash>` or `clawta/<driver>-<ticket-hash>` — and open PRs with auto-generated bodies like `Closes ticket t_XXXX (dispatched via clawta to codex)`. The lifecycle script picks these up and marks tickets `done` on merge. Older docs and some runbooks reference an `agent/` prefix; that requires running the lifecycle script with `--prefix agent/`.

## Operator UI + bus

- [`apps/chitin-console/`](apps/chitin-console/): Angular operator UI, mobile-first, served via Tailscale at `http://100.115.89.9:7878/` — board, threads, inbox, suggestions, achievements.
- [`apps/chitin-console-api/`](apps/chitin-console-api/): Node API backing the console — kanban + agent-bus reads/writes, suggestion routing.
- Agent-bus: SQLite (`~/.chitin/agent-bus.sqlite`) + MCP server (HTTP on `:7878`) + Discord bidirectional mirror. Threads are first-class — cross-agent decisions and standups live there per Constitution §8.

## Observations and research

- [docs/observations/](docs/observations/): dated capture/finding logs from real runs
- [docs/observations/research/](docs/observations/research/): soul archetype surveys, factor analyses
- [docs/observations/quorums/](docs/observations/quorums/): quorum decision records
- [docs/observations/governance-debt-ledger.md](docs/observations/governance-debt-ledger.md): tracked governance debt
